bug_report.summary: Word/character counters inconsistent between before/after for identical Markdown content (includes footnote refs/defs)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new blank Markdown document.
Paste the provided Markdown containing stacked footnote definitions and a trailing body paragraph.
Observe the word count/character count in the UI (top-right counter and/or bottom status bar for the active pane).
expected_behaviors: For identical document content, word and character counts should match between builds.
actual_behaviors: Counts differ between before vs after (e.g., top-right shows 18 words vs 20 words; bottom status shows 29 words / 187 characters vs 31 words / 204 characters), indicating inconsistent stats calculation and/or extra hidden characters being introduced.

bug_report.summary: Invalid footnote-like label with spaces does not fall back to normal link; link destination gets swallowed in preview
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new blank Markdown document.
expected_behaviors: The text renders as a normal hyperlink using the label `^bad id` (or `[^bad id]`) pointing to `https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved`.
The link destination should not disappear from rendering/AST; the preview should reflect a valid clickable link.
actual_behaviors: Preview shows only `This should be a link: ^bad id` and the URL/destination is not rendered/associated with the link (destination appears to be swallowed).
Rendered label formatting differs (brackets removed) and the output is not a normal link to the URL.

bug_report.summary: Valid footnote reference followed by (url) is rendered incorrectly in preview: URL disappears and reference formatting/line break changes
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a Markdown document in Zettlr.
Enter: `This must stay a footnote reference (not a link): [^valid_ref](https://www.wikipedia.org/)` then a blank line, then `[^valid_ref]: This is the footnote body.`
Switch to rendered/preview view (or view the preview pane).
expected_behaviors: `[^valid_ref]` is treated as a footnote reference (not parsed as a normal link label).
The `(https://www.wikipedia.org/)` portion remains visible as literal text (it may be auto-linked as a URL, but must not be consumed as a link destination for `[^valid_ref]`).
The preview keeps the inline structure (no unexpected line break splitting the reference from the preceding text).
actual_behaviors: In preview, the `(https://www.wikipedia.org/)` text is missing entirely, and the reference is rendered as `^valid_ref` with altered formatting/line break (appearing on its own line).
Word/character counters also drop accordingly (consistent with missing URL text).
